**I would like to chat about this before I post any time entries here.**

projects.name: Hardware & Lifeline for Bow of the Tender
Oil Leak From the Transmission Housing Gaskets
projects.notes: Oil of Different colors found on oil absorbent pads beneath the bell housings of both mains.

scopeTasks.desc:
Inspection found that the leak is coming from the gasket where the two halves of the transmission are sealed together. The discrepancy in the color of the oil is from contaminants downhill from the oil weeping. The option for repair is to pull the transmissions and replace the gasket. This is not reasonable at this point. 

Monitor quantity of oil on the oil absorbent pads under the transmission housings as well as the oil on the dipsticks.

projects.name: Stainless Railing, Hawse Pipe and Cavil Cleat Polish Corossion
Replace Tides Shaft Seal Port Main Engine
Sealant Deterioration or New Sealant Bead Needed
projects.notes:
Areas with sealant touch up:
1-Below the teak cap rails in the cockpit
2-Transom above the varnished teak name board just inboard of the transom door.
3-A small section around the teak eyebrow above the windshield
4-Inside the transom door, inside edge around the frame to keep the door sealed up.
5-All around the port aft europa stanchion.
6-A few sections around the upper deck eyebrow on the transom

projects.name: Breaker Panel Lights AC & DC
projects.notes: Breaker Panel indicator lights reported as inoperable. Check lights for functionality repair as needed.

projects.name: Fire Suppression System Service & Certification
projects.notes:
*The handheld extinguishers hit there 5 year service window and each needed to be opened up and serviced. 
**Next year the Firebox canister will need to be removed from the vessel for service. It is on a 7 year service cycle.

projects.name: A/C Engine Room Light Fixture Secure to Overhead
Salon Door Rubbing on Layers of New Varnish

timeEntries.desc:
Removed the engine oil and oil filters from all engines, removed the transmission oil and raw water zincs from the main engines, inspected and cleaned the transmission oil screens, installed new raw water impellers in the main engines using SuperLube, removed and cleaned the air filters for both main engines. Removed the genset sea strainer lid, cleaned the corrosion at the seal, cleaned strainer and applied Super Lube to the seal to prevent leaking.

Removed rust from a section of hard piping on the port main engine and applied a coat of Corroseal.

Buffed gelcoat between cap rail and upper rub rail, port hull mid-salon to aft. Two to three passes fine compound. Applied coat of marine wax to entire hull. Pulled tape from vessel used for buff and wax.
Removed hardware to pull flybridge sliding hatch to replace broken rollers.
Unloaded nearly 50ft of anchor chain, noticed paint markings needed touching up.

Installed new duckbills in overboard discharge pump.
Installed new duckbill inside the Masterflush head. 

Labeled installations and disinfected work areas.
Loaded tie-down lines in buckets with Oxiclean and hot water to soak. Started pressure washing — marine growth would not come off, returned lines to buckets to soak.
Loaded docklines in buckets with bleach and hot water — OxiClean did not fully remove marine growth.
Picked up new LED nav lights and adhesive remover from LFS.
Replaced both navigation light bulbs below the Starlink with LED bulbs.

Removed forward/aft/top genset enclosure panels with peeling insulation. Scraped old insulation, stripped remaining adhesive with 3M Adhesive Remover. Cleaned panels with acetone. Cut and installed new insulation on aft panel, secured fraying seams with butyl tape.

Removed corrosion from all underwater zinc contact points using wire wheel, masked zincs for bottom paint, installed ensuring proper metal-to-metal contact.
Picked up new engine zincs
Created haul estimate per Abe's request.
and painting supplies from LFS.
Masked off bottom paint and zinc mounts on vessel to prep for new coat.

Inspected circuit breaker lights for Proper Operation— refrigeration and electronics dim, genset indicator light is the only bulb not functional. Spoke with Keith and decided that the work required to get the genset light functional was not worth it at this juncture.
Inspected all stainless steel on vessel — cavil cleats and hawseholes needed attention.
Laid out additional 50ft of anchor chain, filled barrel with warm water, scrubbed painted chain markings, set out to dry.
Traced old insulation from top panel — five access ports. Cut and installed insulation on top and forward panels, reinstalled all panels, secured raw water hoses through top panel.
Hung painted chain markings on first 100ft. Mixed InterProtect 2000E epoxy with red coloring agent, applied coat to red markings.

Cleaned all bottom paint below waterline with acetone, applied new coat to approximately three-quarters of hull.

Relocated boat stands in order to paint in the shadows of the stands.  
Painted transom, thru-hulls, thruster tunnel, rudders, and remaining quarter of hull. Pulled tape.
Laid doors on blanket, labeled teak strip locations, removed teak strips for Brightside Restoration. Removed nearly 50 fasteners.

Installed new oil, fuel, and crankcase vent filters for all engines. Installed a new impeller in the generator. Pumped new oil into the engines and topped off the transmission oil. Tapped the damaged threads for all engine zincs and installed new zincs. Labeled all installation dates and cleaned the work area with Simple Green degreaser.
Mixed yellow and red epoxy batches. Applied yellow to painted sections. Applied pink epoxy to insides of links on red-marked sections to seal chain.
Applied coat of red Omni paint to anchor chain markings.

timeEntries.proj: Replace Tides Shaft Seal Port Main Engine
timeEntries.desc: Removed port Tides Marine shaft seal, inspected and cleaned shaft — noted early signs of pitting where the seal had been riding/leaking. Repositioned Tides housing on the shaft log 1/2 inch forward of where it had been. Installed new shaft seal, reassembled housing.
Buffed forward face of flybridge brow — three to four passes coarse compound.
Buffed and waxed forward flybridge brow — two passes fine compound, one coat marine wax.
Buffed top surface of hood forward of windshield — three passes coarse compound.

Removed all fire extinguishers from the vessel for service by Pacific Rim Fire. The handheld extinguishers hit there 5 year service window and each needed to be opened up and serviced. One of the 5lb extinguishers needed to be replaced. 

Purchased fire extinguisher and mount for tender. Installed mount to secure the extinguisher to under the bench seat. 

Loaded certified extinguishers back aboard vessel. 

*Next year the Firebox canister will need to be removed from the vessel for service. It is on a 7 year service cycle.
Evacuated gear oil from windlass, cleaned site glass, filled with new oil.
Buffed and waxed hood forward of windshield. Two passes fine compound, one coat marine wax on top. Buffed starboard side two passes fine compound one coat marine wax port/starboard/forward sides.

Cut UHMW slide, picked up custom stainless fasteners. Installed UHMW slide, filled old broken holes with structural epoxy. Installed both slider doors. 

Bedded the aft end caps on with sealant. 

Installed teak from sliders that had been varnished.

timeEntries.proj: Salon Door Rubbing on Layers of New Varnish
timeEntries.desc:
Repaired salon door — rubbing on buildup of varnish coats.
removed fasteners that hang the door and re-hung the door to allow a clean slide.
Additional door installation and adjustment.

timeEntries.proj: Sealant Deterioration or New Sealant Bead Needed
timeEntries.desc: Removed 7 sections of sealant, cleaned with acetone and taped off in preparation for sealant.

Removed master cabin bed. Checked bow thruster oil levels. Found what looked like baking soda on one side of the compartment vacuumed compartment. Opened up the floor of the compartment to gain full access to the compartment with the thruster. Inspected for any signs of abnormal wear and tear. Everything looks good and dry. Installed missing lug cover on thruster cabling. Reassembled compartments and returned mattress and bedding.

timeEntries.proj: A/C Engine Room Light Fixture Secure to Overhead
timeEntries.desc: Repositioned and secured starboard engine room A/C light fixture — stripped fastener, relocated 1/2 inch aft.

Applied oil to the air filters for the main engines, installed and labeled the service dates on all belts and air filters.

Checked the oil levels in all engines, removed the main engine belt guards to inspect the belts, and performed a final inspection to ensure all procedures were properly completed for the engine services.

Applied a coat of InterProtect 2000E epoxy barrier coat to the section of hard piping on the port main engine that was recently cleared of corrosion.

Loaded a full set of engine filter spares aboard.

Removed contents from lazarette in order to gain access to the rudders. Vacuumed debris from this lower compartment.
Removed rudder feedback and autopilot sensors from the rudder arms. Removed the rudder arms and the collars. Slid the rudders out of the hull. Inspect the bearings. 
Cleaned rudder shafts and shaft logs of old grease. Lubricated shafts with Triple Guard waterproof grease, reinstalled rudders, vacuumed and degreased rudder compartment.
Installed rudder shaft collars, rudder arms, and sensors. Tested steering and rudder angle indicator. Replaced grates and gear into the lazarette as it was found.
Assisted installing flybridge sliding doors. Installed missing port salon teak deck trim fastener.

Installed docklines and fenders, loaded all canvas aboard.

Tested all systems after launch. 

Reinstalled Starlink and antennas post launch.

Delivered vessel to Squalicum Harbor slip.
Unloaded 200ft of anchor chain, recorded markings after hearing they were painted incorrectly. Loaded chain back prior to launch.

timeEntries.proj: Sealant Deterioration or New Sealant Bead Needed
timeEntries.desc: Installed new sealant in seven locations on vessel. Cleaned all sections with acetone, removed tape, recorded photos.
Ran the engines after vessel launch. Checked fluid levels, and topped off the engine and transmission oil.
Cleaned lower deck raw teak and flybridge steps with special 2 part teak cleaner.
Pressure washed nonskid decks, all canvas, and all marine growth. Sprayed flybridge deck with vinegar solution.

timeEntries.proj: Hardware & Lifeline for Bow of the Tender
timeEntries.desc: Picked up 316 stainless bolts, nuts and acorn nuts for tender bow cradle thru deck mount
Washed the vessel when deck clean was complete.

Disposed of fuel in the tender fuel tank and replaced it with Ethanol Free fuel and treated it with Sea-Bil fuel treatment and re-installed the tank. 

Ran tender to flush fresh water through outboard, ran treated fuel through outboard. 
Recorded outboard make/model/serial and make/model of fuel water separator for outboard. Picked up 2x service kits including a raw water pump rebuild kit for the outboard. Went to service outboard and realized that there really weren't enough hours to justify servicing and replacing the filters. Next off season the outboard should be serviced.
Inspected all 4 cutlass bearings for signs of wear or abnormality.

Removed sealant from under the teak cap rail and allowed to dry for a week. I think it worth noting that there was more sealant found between the cap rail and the hull than I expected. This leads me to believe that this was an older issue than we had understood. 

Inspected the bulge at the cap rail very carefully. The bulge is not creating any further damage. My conclusion is that the best way to move forward is to repair the varnish and seal it up well and monitor for any new developments. It would be costly to tear into the teak rail or the fiberglass stanchion.

Taped off new varnish and hull and filled space between the cap rail and the hull with 3m 4000UV.
Removed aft nav light for replacement.

timeEntries.proj: Hardware & Lifeline for Bow of the Tender
timeEntries.desc: Removed hardware, replaced Lifeline Hardware with fitted length (not bottomed out threads). Removed thru deck hardware on the port edge of the deck to inspect the sate of the hardware. Replaced with 316 stainless bolts and bedded into the upper deck.

timeEntries.proj: Starboard Main Engine - Fuse / Start Circuit
timeEntries.desc: Turned on 120v Engine Block Heaters. Checked for A/C voltage in the starter solenoid circuit when starting the engine as well as at rest. Found no A/C voltage on the DC starter circuit. Tracked DC amperage in the Starter solenoid circuit in question and found no more than 26amps at any time during the start up process with or without the A/C engine block heaters on.

With the generator enclosure panels off carefully inspected the top portion of the high pressure fuel pump. Found that the weeping fuel looks to be coming from one of the high pressure lines of the fuel pump. Spoke with mechanic about the issue and concluded that the rate at which the weep is occurring is so low that it is not of concern presently. Will take the next opportunity available to rebuild the fuel pump. If that is next winter that is just fine.

timeEntries.proj: Sealant Deterioration or New Sealant Bead Needed

timeEntries.desc: Installed P-clamps to secure Fireboy deploy cable that was hanging down over the starboard main engine.
Tested Fireboy engine shut down, bypass and reset.

Met Hannah Mae at the dock for haul out and went over the service list for the month. 
Inspections on haul out before pressure wash. 

Removed the Starlink and antennas to ensure the vessel would fit through the shop door prior to launch.

Pulled oil samples from all engines and transmissions before the oil cooled and settled. completed sample documentation and submitted samples for analysis. 

Created a list of supplies needed to service the engines and ensure a full set of spares is available onboard.

Oil Analysis Kits
  x5
Removed all marine grown on the hull below the waterline, as well as all underwater zincs.
Buffed the white gelcoat on the starboard hull below the upper rub rail, extending from the forward end of the vessel to the mid-salon area. Two to three passes with fine compound.

Buffed the green stripe on the starboard hull, the white gelcoat below the upper rub rail extending from the mid-salon area to the aft end of the vessel starboard side, and the gelcoat in-between the cap rail and upper rub rail on the starboard side extending from the aft end of the vessel to the front of the pilothouse. Two to three passes with fine compound.

Buffed the gelcoat in-between the cap rail and upper rub rail on the starboard hull extending from the front of the pilothouse to the forward end of the vessel, along with the white gelcoat below the upper rub rail on the port hull extending from the forward end of the vessel to the mid-salon area. Two to three passes with fine compound.
Replaced FW filter element. There were 2 spares aboard, used one of them.
